title: MUS 347 Quiz 2
instructions: Work through each question on your phone or laptop. Listen to audio examples before answering, then submit for instant local grading.

questions.prompt: Which processor is designed to reduce dynamic range after a signal crosses a threshold?
questions.hint: Look for the tool that turns peaks down once they get too loud.
questions.explanation: A compressor narrows the gap between loud and soft material by reducing peaks above the threshold.

questions.prompt: You want to remove sub-bass rumble from a vocal recording while preserving most of the voice. Which move is the best first step?

choices.label: Apply a high-pass filter around the low end
Reverse the polarity of the track

questions.hint: Think about the filter that removes lows below a cutoff point.
questions.explanation: A high-pass filter is the standard first move for cleaning rumble and handling noise that lives below the useful vocal range.

questions.prompt: Listen to the clip. Which range best describes the tone you hear?
questions.hint: The example sits close to a familiar tuning reference.

media.title: Audio example
media.caption: A short test tone for frequency-range recognition.

questions.explanation: The clip is centered at roughly 440 Hz, which sits in the midrange and matches the familiar concert A reference.

questions.prompt: Which statement about stereo imaging is most accurate?

choices.label: Stereo width is created by differences between left and right channels
A mono file always sounds wider than a stereo file
Stereo width requires reverb on every track
Panning only affects volume, never placement

questions.hint: Think about how the two speakers differ when an image feels wide.
questions.explanation: Stereo width depends on channel differences in timing, level, tone, or phase, while mono collapses those differences.

questions.prompt: This clip is dominated by low-frequency energy. Which tool would most directly tame it while leaving higher content available?
questions.hint: Pick the filter that removes lows below a cutoff frequency.

media.title: Low-end example
media.caption: A short low-frequency tone that stands in for unwanted rumble.

questions.explanation: A high-pass filter removes low-frequency buildup while preserving the mid and high information above the cutoff.
